bird.name: 园林莺
bird.summary: 庭园林莺（學名：Sylvia borin，英語：Garden Warbler，台湾作園林鶯），是雀形目鶲科鶯屬下的一種鳥類。這個物種為植食性，廣泛棲息於擁有豐富的底層植被的灌木地、林地中。在夏季時，這個物種於歐洲繁殖，到了冬季時則遷徙至撒哈拉沙漠以南過冬。園林鶯以其歌聲聞名，是…

locations.name: 理查德·鮑德勒·夏普
locations.summary: 理查德·鮑德勒·夏普（英語：Richard Bowdler Sharpe，1847年11月22日—1909年12月25日）是一位英國動物學家兼鳥類學家，於大英博物館擔任鳥類標本的策展人。在其職涯裡，夏普出版了一些有關鳥類的專著，並整理出博…

locations.name: 撒哈拉以南非洲
locations.summary: 撒哈拉以南非洲（英語：Sub-Saharan Africa）指撒哈拉沙漠以南的非洲地區，也稱黑非洲（Negro Africa）。自然地理上與衣索比亞界高度重疊。政治上，包括了所有或部分领土位于撒哈拉沙漠以南的国家（苏丹除外，尽管位于撒哈拉…

locations.name: 葉尼塞河
locations.summary: 葉尼塞河（俄語：Енисе́й，羅馬化：Yenisey），古稱劍河、謙河，是流入北冰洋最大的河流、世界第五长河流。起源於蒙古國，朝北流向喀拉海，其流域範圍包含了西伯利亞中部大部分地區。从小叶尼塞河河源算起，长4,102公里。以大叶尼塞河河…

locations.name: 阿尔卑斯山
locations.summary: 阿尔卑斯山（德語：Alpen [ˈalpn̩] ；義大利語：Alpi [ˈalpi]；法語：Alpes [alp]；斯洛維尼亞語：Alpe [ˈáːlpɛ]；羅曼什語：Alps）是歐洲次高及橫跨範圍最廣的山脈，它覆蓋了意大利北部邊界、法國…

locations.name: 地中海
locations.summary: 地中海是一個位於歐洲南面、非洲北面以及亞洲西面的陸間海。东西长约4,000公里，南北最宽处大约为1,800公里，面積251.6万平方公里，是地球上最大的陸間海。地中海的平均深度是1,500米，最深處为5,267米。 地中海西部通过直布羅陀…

locations.name: 科西嘉
locations.summary: 科西嘉（法語：Corse，法語發音：[kɔʁs] ；科西嘉语、義大利語：Corsica）是地中海西部的一座岛屿，也是法国的一个单一领土集体及18个大区之一，位于法国大陆部分的东南面，意大利半島的西面，最临近的地块是撒丁岛。科西嘉岛三分之二…

relations.subject: 园林莺
relations.evidence: woodwardi (Sharpe, 1877) }} The garden warbler (Sylvia borin) is a common and widespread small bird that breeds in most of Europe and in the Palearctic to western Siberia

relations.subject: 园林莺
relations.evidence: The garden warbler is strongly migratory, wintering in sub-Saharan Africa

relations.subject: 园林莺
relations.evidence: Deciduous woodland is the preferred breeding habitat.|thumb|alt=A woodland glade The garden warbler breeds in most of Europe between the isotherms and east across temperate Asia to the Yenisei River in Siberia

relations.subject: 园林莺
relations.evidence: All populations are migratory, wintering in sub-Saharan Africa as far south as South Africa. Birds from central Europe initially migrate to the southwest, reorientating to the south or southeast once in Africa, although Scandinavian migrants may head south through the Alps and across the Mediterranean Sea

relations.subject: 园林莺
relations.evidence: All populations are migratory, wintering in sub-Saharan Africa as far south as South Africa. Birds from central Europe initially migrate to the southwest, reorientating to the south or southeast once in Africa, although Scandinavian migrants may head south through the Alps and across the Mediterranean Sea

relations.subject: 园林莺
relations.evidence: woodwardi winters in eastern and southern Africa. Movements in Africa are poorly known, although at least some birds return to the same location in subsequent years. There are only a handful of records of birds recorded in Europe in winter, from Corsica, the UK and Ireland

relations.subject: 园林莺
relations.evidence: Despite a small population decline in much of its European range, the bird's breeding distribution is expanding northwards in Scandinavia
relations.object_summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。

relations.subject: 园林莺
relations.evidence: There is no evidence of any serious decline in numbers, so it is classified by the International Union for Conservation of Nature as being of least concern. There has been a slight decline in numbers in Europe since 1980, although the Scandinavian population is growing. Climate change appears to be affecting the migration pattern of the garden warbler and blackcap
relations.object_summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。
